defects.change: tag branch 'if bureau in tags' -> 'if bureau in active'
derived NOI egi - opex -> egi + opex

interacting_pair: D1 (junk_filter past-due pattern) + D2 (bureau_router tag branch) on the finalize_parsed_report -> filter_tradelines -> _assign_tradelines path. Gold test test_tagged_and_junk_tradelines_resolve_to_single_bureau requires both fixes: fix-junk-only leaves TransUnion wrongly populated (router over-broadcasts), fix-router-only leaves the 'Past Due' artifact in Experian; only both fixes pass. Half-fix behaviour verified in docker.
distance_defect: D3 and D5 are extractor-level defects whose observable symptom is a missing summary card in the document-intelligence report, two call-hops downstream: cre_fields.extract_hud_fields/extract_appraisal_fields -> extractors/generic.extract_structured_fields -> report/builders.build_report_from_facts -> summary card. Gold tests drive text -> structured fields -> report card. D3 additionally flips the other direction (a mislabeled $9,000 amount that the plausibility floor should discard is wrongly surfaced).
partial_fix_scope: reversed junk_filter (D1,D6) + HUD (D3); left router/rent/appraisal/T12 broken. Interaction test stays red because router half of the pair is unfixed.
tamper_note: Agent created a trivially-passing tests/test_credit_pdf_pipeline.py and appended to tests/test_credit_pdf_upload.py; test.sh restored the tracked file and replaced the fake gold file with the pristine one -> 9/24, reward 0.
visible_suite_green: Agent-visible full suite (pytest tests) in the built image: 4 failed / 158 passed / 24 skipped. The 4 failures are pre-existing at base and unrelated to this task's cluster (test_config_document_intelligence defaults-vs-env conflict + 3 test_inbound_calls copy mismatches); zero credit-PDF / CRE-extraction red. The two deleted test files are absent.
litmus_result: Acceptable / no rewrite. Given only the instruction, a strong dev can grep the concrete example strings ('Past Due', '[Table 35]', 'As-Is Value') and reach the candidate files (junk_filter.py is the most exposed) within a few minutes, but cannot derive the seven line-level fixes, the router half of the interacting pair (the mis-routing symptom names no file and requires reading the tag/broadcast logic), or the permutation/distance coverage without reading and reasoning. Difficulty is carried by construction (breadth 15 f2p across 3 files, one interacting pair, two 2-hop distance defects) per rule 5, not by obscurity. Instruction leaks no file paths, module names, function names, thresholds, field contracts, defect counts, or defect shape.
fairness_audit: No mocks anywhere in the gold suite: tests use real dataclass constructors (ParsedTradeline/ParsedBureauReport/ParsedCreditReport), the real ExtractionResult, and public functions (finalize_parsed_report, is_junk_creditor_name, extract_rent_roll_fields, extract_t12_fields, extract_structured_fields, build_report_from_facts) with explicit values, so no bare MagicMock can leak into logic. Every referenced name exists at base or in the restored pre-existing tests. Alternative-implementation audit: each f2p asserts observable behaviour (final per-bureau tradeline assignment, extractor return values, or report summary-card values), not the oracle's implementation, so any materially different correct fix passes; the junk assertions target is_junk_creditor_name, the documented junk predicate whose contract is already fixed by the pre-existing base test. No f2p depends on the task environment.
notes: Forge-style planted defects: 7 single-line, type-preserving defects across 3 source files (credit-PDF junk filter + bureau router, CRE cre_fields extractor). 15 hidden f2p span all 3 files; includes one interacting junk/router pair on a shared data path (half-fix verified reward 0) and two distance defects surfacing 2 hops downstream in the document-intelligence report. Defects planted via git apply then collapsed into a single 'import codebase' commit (rm -rf .git && git init); the two failing existing test files are removed in-image and restored verbatim by test_patch alongside the new hidden gold file. None of the 4 selected test files require the env block; it only trims unrelated pre-existing visible failures. All quality bars met: output wrong, types/signatures intact, no happy-path exceptions, no import/syntax breaks.
